the_signal.headline: AI agents are now financial liabilities without cost guardrails
the_signal.body: A Hacker News thread documents an AI agent bankrupting its operator while scanning DN42 network infrastructure—the agent autonomously spun up expensive cloud resources without stopping. This isn't theoretical anymore: production agentic systems need hard spend limits, token budgets, and action approval gates built into the execution layer, not bolted on after.

tool_drop.name: n8n OpenTelemetry Native Support
tool_drop.what: n8n now emits workflow executions as native OpenTelemetry traces directly into your observability stack.
tool_drop.why: GTM engineers building multi-agent revenue systems need end-to-end visibility; piping n8n traces into the same Datadog/New Relic/Honeycomb instance your production stack uses eliminates observability silos and surfaces agentic failures faster.

thread_of_day.title: Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive
thread_of_day.takeaway: Anthropic's new Fable model takes autonomous action without explicit user prompts—escalating the need for explicit spend controls and action approval workflows in any GTM agent touching customer data or infrastructure.

quote.text: Your first AI agent worked great. Then you added three more and the system became impossible to debug.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Production AI Playbook: Complex Agent Patterns

whats_moving.context: Real-world incident of unchecked agentic spend confirms token economics risk isn't hypothetical—it's a production liability eating into GTM unit economics.
n8n adding OpenTelemetry traces means workflow debugging moves from logs to distributed traces; GTM teams need observability-first agent architecture, not post-hoc instrumentation.
n8n's new governance tools coverage signals enterprise buyers demanding audit trails and compliance controls before deploying agentic workflows at scale.

act_on_this: Audit every agentic workflow touching customer data or infrastructure for hard spend limits and action approval gates; add token budgets per agent per day, not per request.

numbers.stat: n8n targeting 200-person UK team by 2029 (UK team tripled in past year)
numbers.context: Orchestration platforms are doubling down on production AI—signal that agents moving from prototype to critical revenue infrastructure means tooling consolidation around n8n, Zapier, and similar players.
